project: Google & Microsoft Integration V2 — Agent-Grade Workspace Intelligence
description: Complete overhaul of Google integration + net-new Microsoft 365 integration. Centralized provider-abstracted client, 5 AI background jobs (email classification, reply gaps, attendee enrichment, communication health, document linking), subscription tier gating, and proposal-to-Drive storage.

userStories.title: Provider-Abstracted Client Interface
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a single WorkspaceClient interface that abstracts Google and Microsoft so that all edge functions and background jobs work identically across providers.
acceptanceCriteria: _shared/workspaceClient.ts created with provider interface: google | microsoft
Exports createWorkspaceClient(provider, userId, supabase) factory function
Interface covers email, calendar, drive, contacts namespaces with typed methods
Provider-specific implementations in _shared/providers/google.ts and _shared/providers/microsoft.ts
Microsoft provider stubs return not_implemented errors
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Foundation story — all other stories depend on this interface

userStories.title: Centralized Token Refresh
userStories.description: As a user, I want my Google/Microsoft tokens to never expire mid-action so that API calls always succeed.
acceptanceCriteria: _shared/tokenManager.ts created with getValidToken(provider, userId, supabase)
Checks expires_at with 5-minute buffer, refreshes if within buffer
Uses database row-level lock (FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED) to prevent race conditions
All 5 existing Google edge functions refactored to use getValidToken()
Duplicated refreshAccessToken() removed from all edge functions
Drive token refresh now works (was TODO)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Fixes pain points #1 (duplicated refresh) and #2 (Drive TODO)

userStories.title: Auto-Pagination Helper
userStories.description: As a developer, I want pagination handled automatically so that sync operations fetch complete datasets.
acceptanceCriteria: _shared/pagination.ts created with paginateAll(fetchPage, options) helper
Supports Google nextPageToken and Microsoft @odata.nextLink patterns
Configurable maxPages (default 50) and delay (default 100ms)
Gmail sync refactored to use paginateAll() — no longer capped at 10 messages
Calendar list refactored to use paginateAll()
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Fixes pain point #4 (Gmail sync capped at 10)

userStories.title: Typed Error Classification
userStories.description: As an agent, I want typed error responses so I know whether to retry, re-authenticate, or escalate.
acceptanceCriteria: _shared/workspaceErrors.ts with error classes: TokenExpiredError, InsufficientScopeError, RateLimitError, NotFoundError, ProviderError
Each error includes retryable, statusCode, provider fields
Edge functions return correct HTTP codes: 401/403/429/404/500
Error body: { error, code, retryable, provider }
All 5 Google edge functions refactored to use typed errors
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Nylas Routing in Centralized Client
userStories.description: As a user on the free plan, I want Gmail read operations to automatically route through Nylas when connected, and paid users get expanded Nylas operations (send, reply, forward).
acceptanceCriteria: WorkspaceClient.email.list() checks Nylas connection before routing
Nylas connected: routes read ops through Nylas API
Paid tier direct: routes through Gmail API
Free + no Nylas: returns upgrade_required error
Send, reply, forward expanded through Nylas for paid users
nylas-email edge function updated with send, reply, forward actions
Response format identical regardless of routing
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Expands Nylas from read-only to full email operations

userStories.title: Subscription Tier Gating
userStories.description: As a product owner, I want features gated by subscription tier so Basic and Pro plans deliver different value, with free trial getting full Pro.
acceptanceCriteria: _shared/tierGating.ts with getUserTier() and canAccess() functions
Feature matrix config for all gated features
Trial users get full Pro access
Sync frequency resolved per tier: Basic=4h, Pro=30min
History depth resolved per tier: Basic=90d, Pro=unlimited
Gating applied in background job dispatcher
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Microsoft OAuth Flow
userStories.description: As a user, I want to connect my Microsoft 365 account so that 60 can access my Outlook, Calendar, and OneDrive.
acceptanceCriteria: microsoft_integrations table created via migration
microsoft_oauth_states table created via migration
oauth-initiate/providers/microsoft.ts created with PKCE flow
Scopes: Mail.ReadWrite, Mail.Send, Calendars.ReadWrite, Files.ReadWrite, Contacts.Read, User.Read, offline_access
microsoft-oauth-callback edge function created
Redirect URI allowlist matches Google pattern
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser on localhost:5175

userStories.title: Microsoft Token Refresh
userStories.description: As a user, I want my Microsoft tokens to refresh automatically using the centralized token manager.
acceptanceCriteria: tokenManager.ts extended with Microsoft refresh logic
Same race-condition protection (row-level lock)
Same 5-minute expiry buffer
microsoft_integrations.access_token and expires_at updated on refresh
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Microsoft Email Provider (Outlook / Graph API)
userStories.description: As a user with Microsoft connected, I want to send, read, and manage emails through Outlook so the agent works with my Microsoft account.
acceptanceCriteria: microsoft.ts email namespace implemented using Graph API
Actions: list, get, send, reply, forward, draft, archive, trash, star, mark-as-read
Response format mapped to same interface as Google
microsoft-email edge function created
Pagination uses Graph @odata.nextLink via paginateAll()
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Microsoft Calendar Provider
userStories.description: As a user with Microsoft connected, I want calendar events synced from Outlook Calendar so the agent can prep for meetings regardless of provider.
acceptanceCriteria: microsoft.ts calendar namespace implemented using Graph API
Actions: list-events, create-event, update-event, delete-event, list-calendars, availability
Webhook subscriptions via Graph subscriptions endpoint
microsoft-calendar edge function created
Response format mapped to same interface as Google
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Microsoft Drive Provider (OneDrive)
userStories.description: As a user with Microsoft connected, I want OneDrive access so proposals and documents can be stored and linked.
acceptanceCriteria: microsoft.ts drive namespace implemented using Graph API
Actions: list-files, create-folder, upload-file, share-file, get-file, delete-file, search
microsoft-drive edge function created
Response format mapped to same interface as Google
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Microsoft Integration UI
userStories.description: As a user, I want to connect Microsoft 365 from the Integrations page with the same UX as Google.
acceptanceCriteria: Microsoft card added to Integrations page with Outlook logo
Same status badges: active, limited, inactive, error
Connect/disconnect flow mirrors Google
integrationStore.ts extended with microsoft state
checkMicrosoftConnection() and connectMicrosoft() actions added
Service toggles for email, calendar, drive
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser on localhost:5175

userStories.title: Background Job Dispatcher
userStories.description: As a system, I want a central dispatcher that runs background jobs on schedule, respecting user working hours and subscription tiers.
acceptanceCriteria: workspace-background-jobs edge function created
Accepts job_type parameter for all job types
Resolves user working hours from user_settings (default 8am-6pm)
Skips if outside working hours (except token_refresh, calendar_watch)
Checks subscription tier before tier-gated jobs
Processes users in batches of 50 within 60s timeout
Logs to background_job_logs table
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Background Job Scheduling Migration
userStories.description: As a system, I want pg_cron jobs configured to trigger the dispatcher on correct schedules.
acceptanceCriteria: background_job_logs table created via migration
pg_cron and pg_net extensions enabled
10 cron jobs created with correct frequencies per tier
Each cron calls workspace-background-jobs via pg_net
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Proactive Token Refresh Job
userStories.description: As a user, I want my tokens refreshed before they expire so no API call ever fails due to an expired token.
acceptanceCriteria: Queries integrations where expires_at < NOW() + 30 minutes
Refreshes using tokenManager.getValidToken()
Logs success/failure per user
Handles revoked tokens (marks needs_reauth, notifies user)
Runs every 10 minutes regardless of working hours
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Fixes pain point #6. Depends on US-002, US-013

userStories.title: Calendar Watch Auto-Renewal
userStories.description: As a user, I want calendar watches auto-renewed so I never miss meeting notifications.
acceptanceCriteria: calendar_watches table created via migration
Queries watches expiring within 48 hours
Renews Google watches via Calendar API
Renews Microsoft subscriptions via Graph API
Updates expiration in calendar_watches table
Runs daily at 3am UTC regardless of working hours
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Fixes pain point #3. Depends on US-013

userStories.title: Email Sync V2 (Full Paginated)
userStories.description: As a user, I want my full email history synced so the agent has complete context on every contact relationship.
acceptanceCriteria: Email sync uses paginateAll() for complete inbox traversal
Stores sync cursor (historyId for Google, deltaLink for Microsoft)
First sync: 90 days (Basic) or full history (Pro)
Subsequent syncs: incremental from last cursor
email_messages table created via migration with RLS
Schema: id, user_id, provider, message_id, thread_id, from/to/cc, subject, snippet, labels, read, starred, has_attachments, received_at, classification JSONB
Pro: every 30min. Basic: every 4h
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Email Classification Pipeline
userStories.description: As a Pro user, I want incoming emails automatically classified so the agent surfaces buying signals, objections, and urgent items.
acceptanceCriteria: email-classify edge function created
Uses Haiku 4.5 (claude-haiku-4-5-20251001) with prompt caching
Classifies unprocessed emails where classification IS NULL
Schema: intent, sentiment, urgency, deal_relevance, tags, summary
Intents: buying_signal, objection, question, scheduling, follow_up_needed, ooo_auto_reply, newsletter, notification, personal, other
Results stored in email_messages.classification JSONB
Links to contacts via from_email, links to deals via contact-deal
Pro only, hourly during working hours
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Depends on US-017. The killer Pro feature.

userStories.title: Reply Gap Detection
userStories.description: As a user, I want to know which sent emails haven't received a reply so the agent can draft follow-ups proactively.
acceptanceCriteria: reply-gap-detect edge function created
Queries sent emails, checks for reply in same thread
Gap windows: 48h (Pro), 72h (Basic+Pro), 7d (Basic+Pro)
reply_gaps table created via migration
Urgency scoring: 48h on active deal = high, 72h = medium, 7d = low
Clears gap when reply detected
Pro: every 4h. Basic: every 12h
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Sent-vs-Received Ratio Tracking
userStories.description: As a Pro user, I want per-contact communication health metrics so the agent can flag one-directional relationships.
acceptanceCriteria: contact_communication_health table created via migration
Schema: user_id, contact_email, provider, sent/received counts, ratio, last sent/received, avg response time, streak type
Daily rollup from email_messages
Streak detection: 3+ consecutive sends without reply
Links to contact record if exists
Pro only, daily at 2am UTC
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Calendar Attendee Enrichment
userStories.description: As a Pro user, I want meeting attendees automatically enriched with company and role data for comprehensive meeting briefs.
acceptanceCriteria: attendee-enrich edge function created
Triggers on new calendar events via sync or webhook
Creates contact records for unknown attendee emails
Runs lead enrichment pipeline on new contacts
Stores: name, title, company, LinkedIn URL, company size, industry
Works for both Google and Microsoft calendar events
Pro only, every 15 min during working hours
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Depends on US-016. Integrates with existing lead enrichment.

userStories.title: Pre-Meeting Research & Slack Alert
userStories.description: As a Pro user, I want a Slack notification 2 hours before every meeting with a full research brief.
acceptanceCriteria: Extends attendee enrichment to trigger when meeting is within 2 hours
Brief includes: attendee profiles, company intel, deal status, last email, open actions
Formatted as Slack Block Kit message matching existing pattern
Sent to user's Slack DM
Quick-action buttons: View Deal, Draft Agenda, Reschedule
Dedup via meeting_prep_sent flag (triggers once per meeting)
Works for both Google and Microsoft events
Pro only
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Depends on US-021. The Slack alert users already love — now automatic for every meeting.

userStories.title: Proposal Storage to User's Drive
userStories.description: As a user, I want proposals stored in my Google Drive or OneDrive instead of S3.
acceptanceCriteria: Proposal pipeline uploads to user's Drive/OneDrive first
Creates '60 Proposals' folder if not exists
File named: {Company} - Proposal - {Date}.pdf
Auto-shares with proposal recipients
Drive file URL stored in proposals table
Falls back to S3 if no Drive/OneDrive connected
Works for both Google Drive and OneDrive
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Drive Document Linking
userStories.description: As a Pro user, I want recently shared documents auto-linked to deals so the agent can reference them.
acceptanceCriteria: deal_documents table created via migration
Background job scans recent Drive/OneDrive activity (last 2 hours)
Matches docs to deals by: recipient email, file name contains company, created during meeting
Stores in deal_documents table
Agent can query documents shared with a contact/company
Pro only, every 2 hours during working hours
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Integration Dashboard Updates
userStories.description: As a user, I want the Integrations page to show both Google and Microsoft with clear tier-based feature visibility.
acceptanceCriteria: Microsoft 365 card added alongside Google card
Both cards show per-service status (email, calendar, drive)
Pro features show lock icon with Upgrade to Pro for Basic users
Background job status: Last synced X min ago, Classification: N new insights
Communication health summary: N contacts need follow-up
Connected provider count shown
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser on localhost:5175

userStories.title: Email Intelligence UI
userStories.description: As a user, I want classified emails surfaced with intent tags, urgency indicators, and reply gap warnings.
acceptanceCriteria: Email list shows classification badges (buying signal, objection, etc.)
Urgency indicator (red/amber/green dot)
Filter by classification intent
Reply gap warnings inline with draft follow-up CTA
Communication health badge on contact cards
Basic users see Upgrade to Pro placeholder
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser on localhost:5175

userStories.title: Deprecate Legacy Google Functions
userStories.description: As a developer, I want legacy inline token refresh and per-function pagination removed.
acceptanceCriteria: All inline refreshAccessToken() removed from 5 edge functions
All manual pagination loops replaced with paginateAll()
All generic catch return 400 replaced with typed errors
Gmail sync 10-message cap removed
No functional behavior change — existing API contracts preserved
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Final cleanup — depends on US-002, US-003, US-004

userStories.title: Deprecate S3-Only Proposal Storage
userStories.description: As a developer, I want proposals to default to Drive/OneDrive with S3 as fallback only.
acceptanceCriteria: Proposal generation checks Drive/OneDrive first
Connected: uploads to 60 Proposals folder
Not connected: falls back to S3
proposals table updated with storage_provider and drive_file_id columns
Existing S3 URLs continue to work
Typecheck passes
